We are in the Build phase of the Lean Consort Model. We need to extract knowledge packs from the 36 existing agent definitions.

## What exists

The old agents are at:
C:\Users\oscar\AI WORKBENCH\devChorus\automagically-refactored\.opencode\agents\

They have valuable domain knowledge encoded in markdown+YAML frontmatter files.
We need to extract their expertise into structured knowledge packs.

## Your task

Read ALL agent files in .opencode/agents/ and extract their knowledge into structured packs at:
C:\Users\oscar\AI WORKBENCH\devChorus\automagically-refactored\docs\packs\

Create these packs (each as a structured .md file with the format defined in design/lean-consort-model.md):

1. **game-design-pack.md** — from: creative-director, game-designer, systems-designer, level-designer, economy-designer, narrative-director, writer
2. **pixijs-rendering-pack.md** — from: art-director, technical-artist, pixijs-specialist, world-builder
3. **qa-testing-pack.md** — from: qa-lead, qa-tester, performance-analyst, accessibility-specialist
4. **genre-platformer.md** — from: .opencode/templates/genre-patterns/platformer.md

Each pack must follow this format:

`yaml
---
name: pack-name
when: "Paths or contexts where relevant"
triggers: "What should cause a recommendation"
---
`

Then sections:
1. ## When to use
2. ## Constraints
3. ## Patterns
4. ## Anti-patterns
5. ## Checklist
6. ## References
7. ## Escalation

Important: Extract the real domain knowledge from the agents. Do not summarize — preserve actionable patterns, checklists, constraints, and anti-patterns. This is knowledge extraction, not creative writing.

Write the pack files using the Write tool. Do NOT execute any shell commands.
